english: 1. now, at present, dá-ċi-nas (da ci nas) dá-lta (da lta) pán-la (phan la) from lately till now Thgy.; dá-ltai (da lta'i) (or dá-ltar-gyi (da ltar gyi)) bár-du (bar du) until now; dá-ltai (da lta'i) spyód-lam (spyod lam) our course of acting during this life Glr.; dá-ltar-gyi (da ltar gyi) byá-ba (bya ba), or dṅós-po (dngos po) a person's experience or actions during the present period of his life Dzl.; da-lta-nyid-du (da lta nyid du) Glr., da-lta-ráṅ (da lta rang) Mil, Pth., instantly; dá-lta-ba (da lta ba) Cs., dá-ltar-ba (da ltar ba) Gram., dus dá-lta-ba (dus da lta ba) the present time, presence; the present tense 2. W. hereafter, afterwards, *dág-sa* (dag sa) mi gos (mi gos), dál-ta toṅ* (dal ta tong) I do not want it now; give it me afterwards. — dá-ste (da ste) henceforth, from this time forward Dzl. — da-dúṅ (da dung) (frq. pronounced and spelled da-rúṅ (da rung)) v. below. — da-dé (da de) Glr. and C. now. — da náṅ (da nang) this morning. — dá-ni (da ni) 1. now, 2. henceforth Glr. da-pyi(n)-ċad (da phyin chad) Dzl., da-pyis (da phyis) Glr. henceforth. — dá-byuṅ (da byung) a man of yesterday, an upstart. — dá-tsam (da tsam) about this time. — da-tsúr (da tsur) henceforth Pth. — da-yzód (da gzod) but now, but just, not until now. — *da-ráṅs* (da rangs) C. = da-naṅ (da nang). — da-rúṅ (da rung), da-dúṅ (da dung) still, still more, da-rúṅ toṅ (da rung tong) give still more! da-rúṅ légs-par ysúṅ-bar żu (da rung legs par gsung bar zhu) please, explain it more in detail Ma.; still longer, once more, da-rúṅ yaṅ (da rung yang) again and again, over and over again; *da-rúṅ tsá-big ma tsar* (da rung tsa big ma tsar) W. it is not quite finished yet. — da-rés (da res) (Sch. also da-ré-ba? (da re ba?)) 1. now, now at least, but for this time (opp. to sṅán-ċad (sngan chad), sṅar (sngar), pyis (phyis)) Mil. 2. W. formerly, heretofore (opp. to da (da) now). — dá-lo (da lo) this year, in this year.
horse-shoe, *dhá-ǩa gyáb-pa* (dha kha rgyab pa) to shoe a horse C.
(*stá-ċi*? (sta ci?)) sickle hook, for cutting off briers Lh.

wylie: da phrug, dwa phrug, da tse

english: a plant Med., yielding an acrid drug; da-tsód (da tsod) id.(?); da-rgód (da rgod), and da-yyúṅ (da gyung) are two species of this plant, the former of which is considered to be of greater virtue Wdn.
col. and sometimes B. = dár-ba (dar ba) buttermilk.

1. sign of the plural, eleg. for rnams (rnams); often added to the pronouns de (de) and odi ('di), and sometimes to numerals; also in the combination dag-rnams (dag rnams). In translations of Sanskrit works it denotes the dual number. — 2. ṅá-dag (nga dag), k̇yéd-dag (khyed dag), seems in Mil. often to be used for ṅá-lta-bu-dag (nga lta bu dag) my equal, or equals (another reading is ṅá-lta (nga lta), v. lta (lta) 2). — 3. W. col. = da (da), esp. in the compounds *dág-sam* (dag sam), *dág-sa* (dag sa) now; also certainly, it is true (v. da (da) 2) Mil. — 4. v. dág-pa (dag pa).
is said to be used in Ts. for dé-ǩa (de kha).
Lh. mint, aromatic plant, Mentha Royliana.

(prop. pt. of odág-pa ('dag pa)), clean, pure; cleanness, purity; as adj. also dág-po (dag po), W. *dág-mo* (dag mo)*; dág-par gyúr-ba (dag par gyur ba) to become clean, dág-par byéd-pa (dag par byed pa) to make clean, to cleanse, to purify, dág-par ǩrú-ba (dag par khru ba) (W. *dág-mo ṭu-ċe* (dag mo thu ce)*) to wash clean; more frq. fig.: *ǩa ma dhag* (kha ma dhag)* C. impure, incorrect, vulgar pronunciation, cf. sgra (sgra) 1; rigs ma dag (rigs ma dag) impure blood or kindred; com. pure with regard to religion and morals, (also = holy, sacred, relative to lifeless objects), lus daṅ ṅag daṅ yíd-kyi las yóṅs-su dág-pa (lus dang ngag dang yid kyi las yongs su dag pa) quite pure in word and action Dzl.; lus dag sems dag dbáṅ-po dag (lus dag sems dag dbang po dag), also lus-ytsaṅ (lus gtsang) etc. id.; dág-par ts'ó-ba (dag par tsho ba) to lead a pure, a virtuous life; smón-lam dág-pa (smon lam dag pa) is stated to mean a sincere prayer Glr.; rnám-(par) dag(-pa) (rnam (par) dag (pa)) quite pure, most holy, frq.;
